comparison_method: intersect Paper 3 holdout IDs with Paper 2 per-id refusal judgments
rebalance_method: down-sample help-side (overref) to match refuse-side (core_s2 + xl)

meta-llama/Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct.note: loss flat; margins slightly positive but within noise

google/gemma-3-4b-it.note: convergent: loss dropped 12%, margins climbed 24x, in-dist accuracy 0.81

headline_finding: Probe-guided DPO at ~176 preference pairs learns the in-distribution contrast on one of three anchors (Gemma 0.81 accuracy, margins +0.18) but does not generalise to OOD held-out safety dimensions on any anchor. The data scale, not the optimisation, is the bottleneck.
open_questions: Why did only Gemma train at LR=5e-6? Probe-depth interaction (Gemma blocks at depth 0.71-0.79; Qwen/Llama at 0.82-0.94) is the leading hypothesis.
Does Qwen/Llama trained-to-convergence (via mid-depth probe or higher LR) also produce flat OOD? If yes, claim hardens to 'data is the bottleneck'.
